@drewjohnson-85  No they didn't. That is a myth people spread after a CDPR lady who was watching over the project talked about the early production where there characters where being approved. Becca was approved with no issue after they made it clear how the story would go. At the end she said that in hindsight she feels bad she was originally against the character and jokingly stated that Trigger was like "The loli must stay!" which is where the joke comes from.

Since the hate wagon for Cyberpunk was still high at the time the haters twisted the story into "Stupid CDPR wanted to remove the loli but brave Trigger saved ger!".

@GuciomDude I saw the interview, I know just how much of a joke it was, and what context it was said in, your the one spreading a myth, Beca was approved after Trigger explained her role when CDPR asked why she looked like that, because they were vital to the creation process, people tend to make light of the conversation between both companies much like she did because it’s funny, but that doesn’t make the underlying truth that she was trying to convey any less real.

@GuciomAlso I suspect that you haven’t seen any other Studio Trigger projects because the style of storytelling shows hallmark of they’re work, clearly they were heavily involved in every part of the creation process.
